incorrects.description: Student placed wrong product tile on first attempt

steps.text: Not quite. Skip-count by 10s: 10, 20, 30. How many times did you count?

incorrects.description: Student placed wrong product tile on second attempt

params.description: Category A row highlights. 3 symbols pulse sequentially.

steps.text: Count the symbols in the highlighted row. 3 symbols. Each symbol stands for 10. That means 3 groups of 10. Skip-count: 10, 20, 30. Place that product.

incorrects.description: Student placed wrong product tile three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: 30 tile moves into product slot. Equation builder shows 3 × 10 = 30.

steps.text: Let me show you. The row has 3 symbols. Each symbol is worth 10. So we skip-count by 10s three times: 10, 20, 30. 3 times 10 equals 30. That is the product.
